note: Canonical config for the GENERATED SURFACE only: the generator (L6) renders slash-commands + agents + the hook block from this file. The RUNTIME enforcement facts (the gate set, HIGH_RISK={T3,T4} tiers, the 14-state lifecycle) are HARDCODED in the stdlib kernel and are NOT loaded from here at runtime — so the kernel runs even if this file is absent, and this file mirrors rather than drives them. Only dispatch.required_agents is drift-locked to the kernel by a test. JSON (not YAML) so the kernel has zero deps. Edit here, then `zaude gen`.

models.policy: Every agent inherits the driver model (Deen: no sonnet/haiku/opus — use the model I'm using).

commands.name: start
commands.summary: Resume: show current state, allowed next steps, risk tier, open intake.

commands.name: onboard
commands.summary: Scaffold a new project into Zaude (vault + git + .zaude + PM).

commands.name: clarify
commands.summary: Record requirements + acceptance criteria (Intake -> Clarified).

commands.name: prioritize
commands.summary: Record the priority decision (Clarified -> Prioritized).

commands.name: plan
commands.summary: Record the ordered plan (Prioritized -> Planned).

commands.name: design
commands.summary: Record the design + decision id (Planned -> Designed).

commands.name: classify-risk
commands.summary: Set the risk tier T0..T4 (Designed -> RiskClassified). Gates scale to risk.

commands.name: approve
commands.summary: Record approval (RiskClassified -> Approved). Source edits unlock for high-risk work.

commands.name: implement
commands.summary: Mark implementation underway (Approved -> Implemented).

commands.name: test
commands.summary: Record test evidence (Implemented -> Tested). Exit code must be 0.

commands.name: review
commands.summary: Record the review ledger (Tested -> Reviewed). Run the panel first.

commands.name: verify
commands.summary: Record verification evidence (Reviewed -> Verified).

commands.name: shippable
commands.summary: Quality-gate aggregate (Verified -> Shippable).

commands.name: ship
commands.summary: Ship: refuses without a clean review + verification; issues a deploy token (Shippable -> Released).

commands.name: close
commands.summary: Close out (Released -> Closed).

commands.name: build
commands.summary: Autonomous agent-driven build: plan->design->implement->review panel->verify, recorded in the signed trace, driven to tier-4 (done-with-evidence).
commands.body:
Zaude **autonomous build** -- the agent-driven engine (Zaude 3). Give a goal in $ARGUMENTS; this runs the full plan -> design -> build -> review-panel -> verify loop with REAL agents and records every stage in the signed trace, driving to tier-4 (done-with-evidence).

Principle: run AGENTS to do the work; run `zaude` to RECORD it. The signed trace (.zaude/trace.jsonl) is the source of truth -- never hand-edit it. If a kernel call REFUSES (wrong state / gate / missing evidence), satisfy the next legal step it prints or STOP. FULL-AUTO: keep going across stages and items; HARD-STOP only on (a) a destructive/irreversible step, (b) an unresolved CRITICAL or HIGH finding, or (c) genuinely unclear scope.

Let Z = `python "$HOME/.zaude/bin/zaude.py"`.

0. STATE. `Z status`. If nothing is in flight, open the item: `Z pm-add "<goal>"` then advance to Clarified: `Z clarify --note "<requirements + acceptance criteria>"`.
1. PLAN. Dispatch `workflow-orchestrator` to decompose the goal into ordered steps. Record: `Z plan --note "<ordered steps>"`.
2. DESIGN. Dispatch `architect-review` (DESIGN mode); add `design-bridge` + `frontend-developer` for UI and/or `backend-developer` for services per surface; reconcile the contract. Record: `Z design --decision-id <DEC-ID> --text "<the design>"`.
3. RISK. Classify T0-T4 (auth / migration / prod / security / destructive => T3/T4). Record: `Z classify-risk --tier <T>`.
4. APPROVE. T0-T2: `Z approve` and continue. T3/T4: HARD-STOP -- present design + risk and get operator approval before `Z approve`.
5. IMPLEMENT. Dispatch the specialist(s) the work calls for (per 03-patterns/agent-usage.md: python-pro / react-specialist / postgres-pro / etc.) to WRITE the code from the design -- no design decisions here. Record: `Z implement --note "<what changed>"`.
6. TEST. Actually run the tests/build. Record the real command + exit code: `Z test --cmd "<cmd>" --exit <code>`. Exit must be 0 to advance; if not, fix and re-run.
7. REVIEW PANEL (parallel). Claude seats: `code-reviewer`; `security-auditor` (only if the diff touches auth / crypto / SSH / credentials / input validation); `architect-review` (REVIEW). Model-diverse seats (best-effort, never block): Codex `codex exec --dangerously-bypass-approvals-and-sandbox` on the diff, and OpenCode `opencode run` on the diff. Fold findings; count unresolved CRITICAL/HIGH. Record: `Z review --unresolved <n> --codex-verdict <pass|concerns|fail> --opencode-verdict <pass|concerns|fail>`. HARD-STOP if unresolved CRITICAL/HIGH > 0 -- fix, then re-review.
8. VERIFY (live evidence). Inspect the built artifact / run a health or live probe; dispatch `evidence-verifier` to refuse any unproven claim. Record: `Z verify --built <ok> --health <ok> --probe <ok>`.
9. SHIP. `Z shippable`. For a deploy/publish: HARD-STOP and confirm (destructive) before `Z ship`. Otherwise `Z ship` records the release of the deliverable.
10. DoD. `Z dod` -- exit 0 = tier-4 green (done-with-evidence); exit 2 = NOT done, return to the failing stage and continue. Do not stop on "looks done"; only on dod=0. Then `Z close`.

Commits/pushes of the code repo happen only at SHIP or on explicit operator request. Use `/zwaive <reason>` only with a real, logged reason -- never bypass a gate silently.

commands.name: fast
commands.summary: FAST LANE for small/low-risk work: Intake -> Approved in one step; code immediately.

commands.name: fast-ship
commands.summary: FAST SHIP for small work: Approved -> Released in one step. REFUSES if tests didn't pass.

commands.name: waive
commands.summary: Record an explicit, logged waiver to bypass a (waivable) gate.

commands.name: status
commands.summary: Print the current state projection (rebuilt from the signed trace).

commands.name: doctor
commands.summary: Health check: trace integrity + kernel-version drift.

commands.name: repair
commands.summary: Rebuild state.json from the append-only trace (never rewrites the trace).

commands.name: trace-verify
commands.summary: Validate the signed trace (hash-chain + HMAC integrity + legal state replay).

commands.name: dod
commands.summary: Definition-of-Done / autonomous-loop end-condition (tier-4 = done-with-evidence).

commands.name: persona
commands.summary: Operator-learning persona — the distilled 'decide as the operator would' profile loaded first in autonomous mode.

commands.name: codex
commands.summary: Show codex review-seat status (availability + token + retry window). Best-effort; never gates.

commands.name: opencode
commands.summary: Show OpenCode review-seat status (the third, model-diverse best-effort reviewer). Never gates.

commands.name: agents
commands.summary: Report which required capability agents are installed vs MISSING (advisory; install separately).

commands.name: next
commands.summary: Print the next lifecycle command for the current state (autonomous-loop helper; stops at DoD).

commands.name: route
commands.summary: INTENT DETECTION: map a natural-language request to the right command + a safety mode (auto/propose/confirm).

commands.name: remember
commands.summary: Collective memory: append a lesson/fact/decision (redacted, operator-private).

commands.name: recall
commands.summary: Collective memory: retrieve the most relevant entries for a query (TF-IDF).

commands.name: vault-sync
commands.summary: Project the signed trace into the vault: regenerate current-state.md (trace-anchored) + append-only decisions.md. Idempotent.

commands.name: vault-push
commands.summary: Sync the project vault to a shared GitHub repo (own git repo under vault/; best-effort).

commands.name: wrap
commands.summary: End-of-session wrap (v2): final review + vault-sync (trace->vault) + collective-memory lessons + credential scan + best-effort vault-push. Trace stays the source of truth.
commands.body:
Zaude **end-of-session wrap** for a v2 (trace-anchored) project. The signed trace (`.zaude/trace.jsonl`) is the source of truth; `vault/<slug>/current-state.md` is a GENERATED projection — never hand-edit it.

Run these in the project directory, in order. If any kernel command REFUSES (a gate, the wrong state, or failed trace integrity), surface the next legal step it printed and STOP — never bypass a gate or hand-edit `.zaude/`.

1. **Final review (report-only).** If `git status` shows uncommitted changes, run a final `code-reviewer` pass and report findings by severity. Do NOT fix. If CRITICAL or HIGH, STOP and tell the operator before syncing or pushing. (Safety net, not the lifecycle `/zreview` gate.)
2. **Project the trace into the vault:** `python "$HOME/.zaude/bin/zaude.py" vault-sync`. Regenerates `vault/<slug>/current-state.md` and appends trace-anchored entries to `decisions.md`. If it refuses on trace integrity, run `/zrepair` and STOP.
3. **Record lessons to collective memory.** Scan this session for corrections the operator gave, approaches they validated, new project facts (with a why), or pointers to external systems. For each: `python "$HOME/.zaude/bin/zaude.py" remember "<lesson>" --tags "<comma,tags>"`. Redacted + operator-private.
4. **Credential scan.** Scan the session for pasted secrets (passwords, API keys, tokens, SSH keys). Report each as first-4/last-4 only plus the service it belongs to, for rotation. Never echo a full secret; never write one into the vault or the trace.
5. **Push the vault (best-effort):** `python "$HOME/.zaude/bin/zaude.py" vault-push`. No remote / offline is fine — report it as advisory.
6. **Report final state:** `python "$HOME/.zaude/bin/zaude.py" next`, then show the current state + next legal step. Run `git status` on the code repo and report clean/dirty honestly — do NOT commit or push the code repo unless the operator asked.

commands.name: runner
commands.summary: Emit CI workflow + (self-hosted) a VPS runner-install script. --mode github|self-hosted.

commands.name: intake
commands.summary: Drop a rough idea into your Intake column.

commands.name: promote
commands.summary: Promote an intake idea into a Feature (user story + AC + child tasks/bugs).

commands.name: board
commands.summary: Show the local board (Intake + Backlog).

commands.name: pm-init
commands.summary: Provision the GitHub Projects v2 board (repo + Intake-first columns + labels + fields).

commands.name: pm-sync
commands.summary: Push the board to GitHub (idempotent).

commands.name: pm-pull
commands.summary: Reconcile PM edits from GitHub back into the trace (PM-wins for business fields).

commands.name: pm-mirror
commands.summary: Mirror the board to vault/<slug>/backlog.md + the memory index.

agents.name: evidence-verifier
agents.brief: Refuse any 'done'/'resolved'/'shipped' claim that is not backed by APPLY EVIDENCE: a real command + its exit code, the built artifact actually inspected (grep the deployed bundle, not the source), a live health/probe result. Output a verification.json {built_artifact_check, health, live_probe, exit_codes}. If evidence is missing, say so plainly and name what's needed. Never accept LLM recollection as evidence.

agents.name: supply-chain-auditor
agents.brief: Audit dependencies + provenance before ship: new/changed deps, known-critical vulnerabilities, lockfile integrity, install scripts, typosquats, unpinned versions. Produce an sbom.json summary + a blocking verdict on any known-critical. Flag anything that pulls code at install time.

agents.name: ui-design-implementer
agents.brief: Frontend design/UX is a first-class deliverable, not an afterthought (finding #7). When a feature touches UI: (1) if a screenshot or design reference is provided, reproduce it faithfully — spacing, hierarchy, color, states; codex is strong at reading screenshots and turning them into concrete component/CSS specs, so pair codex (read the design) + claude (implement) here; (2) otherwise apply sane defaults — accessible contrast, loading/empty/error states, responsive layout, focus management, no dead/hardcoded data. Output ui-notes.md: the design rules applied, the components built, and any screenshot/design inputs used. Never ship a placeholder UI.

dispatch.external_reviewers_note: BOTH are advisory/best-effort, NEVER required: a missing/unauth/out-of-credit/crashed external reviewer never blocks /review or /ship. The ship gate reads ONLY unresolved_critical_high.

tiers.T1: small fix — code flows; evidence (tests) before ship
tiers.T2: normal feature — design recorded; review + verify before ship
tiers.T3: risky — full lifecycle + review panel (codex used if available — best-effort, never a gate)
tiers.T4: auth/migration/prod/security/destructive — full gates + panel + approval (codex used if available)

review_panel.rule: Claude lenses always run. Codex and OpenCode are BEST-EFFORT external seats: a missing / unauthenticated / out-of-credit / timed-out / crashed / skipped reviewer NEVER blocks /review or /ship — the cycle continues without it (recorded honestly), and an available-but-unrun reviewer is a non-blocking nudge. The two seats are independent (one's no-credit backoff never affects the other). The ship gate reads ONLY unresolved_critical_high — no seat sets the gate by itself; the driver folds any verdict it wants to honor into that count. OpenCode is provider-agnostic, so it adds a MODEL-DIVERSE third voice (e.g. Gemini/GPT/local).
